ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Report Of Status With A Petition Requesting Additional Time For Estate Administration Must Be Filed At Least Two Weeks Before That Status Date. Appearance Will Be Required On Jun-16-2026 If No Petition Has Been Filed.

TENTATIVE RULING: OFF CALENDAR
EXAMINER: HELEN TROWBRIDGE

Petition For 1) Full Iaea Authority Or In The Alternative To Have Full Iaea Authority Only To Sell Real Estate Worth Up To $10,000.000.00; 2) For Reissuance Of Letters Testamentary; 3) For Bond To Remain At $300,000.00 Or In The Alternative To Deposit Sales Proceeds From Real Property Into A Blocked Account; And 4) That Petitioner May Apply Ex Parte For An Exclusive Listing Agreement To Sell Real Property
Off Calendar Apr-29-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Constance Figuers appeared via zoom for Norma Muldrow (petitioner).

Discussion was held regarding the three properties that want to be sold by Ms. Figuers client, that she would like to pay off the $7,000,000 state tax balance and avoid increasing the current $300,000 bond amount, and that after the sale of the properties the proceeds are to go into a blocked account.

The Court ordered proceeds from the real property sale's to be placed into a blocked account, and bond will remain at $300,000.00. The Court also upgraded the executor's limited IAEA authority to full IAEA authority.

The matter is taken under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-26-2022 Continued To Aug-16-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Konstantine Demiris, via Zoom, for Norma M. Muldrow, Executor.

The court advises counsel that pursuant to probate code 11621(a) the court has determined that distribution cannot be made at this time without loss to creditors or injury to the estate or any interested person. Counsel offers advocacy for the petition and history of the case. Counsel is advised that the court is willing to allow a continuance for him formally file written advocacy with the court or, the court will consider granting distribution but will require a bond. Counsel requests a continuance.

The Petition for Preliminary Distribution is continued to Aug-16-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Counsel is instructed to file his papers by Aug-05-2022.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-29-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Konstantine Demiris, via ZOOM, for Norma Marie Muldrow, via ZOOM, Petitioner.

The court advises that this matter was called today because the decedent executed two wills on the date. One of the wills included a provision gifting $2,000,000 to John Scheid who did not appear today. The court advises Mr. Demiris that it is inclined to grant the petition appointing his client as Executor with limited authority and a bond in the amount of $300,000. Mr. Demiris agrees with the court's tentative ruling. Mr. Demiris notes Mr. Scheid's appearance at the previous calling of this matter and advises that Mr. Scheid has been properly notified of today's hearing.

There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated Oct-08-2020 without including Article Fourth C) is admitted to probate. Norma Marie Muldrow is appointed Executor of the estate with limited authority. Bond is set at $300,000. Karen Ann Simons is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Dec-19-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-01-2021 Continued To Nov-29-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Konstantine Demiris, via ZOOM, for Norma Marie Muldrow, Petitioner; John Scheid, via ZOOM, Beneficiary.

The court advises counsel that it intends to grant the request to extend special administration and asks counsel if he has reviewed the Examiner's notes. Counsel reports that he has submitted a declaration for e-filing that addresses the Examiner's notes. The court advises counsel that it is looking for advocacy and practicality to explain why a blocked account should be allowed instead of a bond.

The order Extending Special Administration and Authorizing the Issuance of Special Letters is signed in open court. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to Nov-29-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

EX PARTE PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION
